verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I am told, to take another point, that one of his followers, Chrysogonus, finds fault with me for having said that in baptism all sins are put away, and, in the case of the man who was twice married, that he had died and risen up a new man in Christ; and further that there were several such persons who were Bishops in the churches.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I will make him a short answer.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: He and his friends have in their hands my letter, for which they take me to task.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Let him give an answer to it, let him overthrow its reasoning by reasoning of his own, and prove my writings false by his writings.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Why should he knit his brow and draw in and wrinkle up his nostrils, and weigh out his hollow words, and simulate among the common crowd a sanctity which his conduct belies?

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Let me proclaim my principles once more in his ears: That the old Adam dies completely in the laver of baptism, and a new man rises then with Christ; that the man that is earthly perishes and the man from heaven is raised up.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I say this not because I myself have a special interest in this question, through the mercy of Christ; but that I made answer to my brethren when they asked me for my opinion, not intending to prescribe for others what they may think right to believe, nor to overturn their resolution by my opinion.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: For we who lie hid in our cells do not covet the Bishop’s office.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: We are not like some, who, despising all humility, are eager to buy the episcopate with gold; nor do we wish, with the minds of rebels, to suppress the Pontiff chosen by God; nor do we, by favouring heretics, show that we are heretics ourselves.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: As for money, we neither have it nor desire to have it.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: “Having food and clothing, we are therewith content;” and meanwhile we constantly chant the words describing the man who shall ascend to the hill of the Lord: “He that putteth not out his money to usury, nor taketh reward against the innocent; he who doeth these things shall not be moved eternally.”

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: We may add that he who does the opposite to these will fall eternally.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Almost every sentence in this last chapter is an insidious allusion to Rufinus.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: His “wrinkled-up brow” and “turned-up nose,” his weighing out his words, his supposed wealth, are all alluded to in other places and especially in the satirical description of him given after his death in Jerome’s letter (cxxv. c.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
